description: SUBSEA MAILiRpAEF Relief from British interference with American mail is in partial de gree at least expected from the postal announcement of a tentative agree ment with the German embassy re garding submarine carrying of mails Correspondence entrusted To the sub sea liner Deutschland is calculated to be delivered within much quicker time than the several months now required for a letter to travel by surface ships subject to delay of British examina tion and censorship The submarine mail carrier offers direct postal communication between Germany and America Providing the Teutonic powers place no such re strictions about war references as Britain is placing there may even be a clearer understanding of the European situation reach this country through the medium of the subsea mail THE ATTACK BN MASSE The official despatch from British field headquarters under date of Oc tober 15 estimated the German death list in the counter attack against the Allied lines at Artois at between 8000 and 9000 men Following a very heavy bombardment the assault made with 28 battalions in the front line and with larger forces in sup port At all points of the line ex cept two the Germans were repulsed with tremendous
